title: M.Sc Forensic Science with specialization in Cyber and Multimedia Forensics
content: The M.Sc. Forensic Science program with a specialization in Cyber and Multimedia Forensics is a postgraduate course designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of forensic science principles, focusing on the investigation of cybercrimes and the analysis of digital and multimedia evidence. This specialization prepares students for careers in digital forensics, cybersecurity, law enforcement, and related fields, equipping them with the necessary skills to handle complex cyber investigations and multimedia evidence analysis. The curriculum combines core forensic science courses with specialized training in cyber and multimedia forensics, supplemented by practical training, laboratory work, internships, and field experiences. Graduates of the M.Sc. Forensic Science with specialization in Cyber and Multimedia Forensics have a wide range of career opportunities, including: Digital Forensic Analyst, Cybersecurity Specialist, Multimedia Forensics Expert. The students after completion of the program are eligible for working as forensic professional in government and private forensic science laboratories. Also, the students can opt for joining law firms, MNCs, insurance companies, banks as forensic consultant. The program offers student a wide are of employment opportunities as it has been developed after taking inputs from the members of the industry. 01 Impart comprehensive knowledge of cybercrime investigation, digital evidence handling, and multimedia forensics, integrating principles of computer science and criminal justice. 02 Train students in the use of advanced tools and techniques for data recovery, network forensics, mobile forensics, image/video analysis, and malware detection. 03 Develop skills to analyze and interpret digital evidence from computers, mobile devices, and cloud platforms in accordance with legal and ethical standards. 04 Encourage research and innovation in emerging areas such as cyber threat intelligence, blockchain forensics, AI-based detection, and digital privacy issues. 05 Prepare graduates for professional roles in cybercrime units, forensic laboratories, government and private cybersecurity firms, and law enforcement agencies. 01 After Completion of the Course, the students can get an employment in the Government Forensic Science Laboratories either in the State FSLs or in the Central FSLs. 02 The students would be equipped with the necessary training and knowledge to work in other firms such as Banks, Insurance Agencies, Detective Agencies, Security Agencies, Pharmaceutical Companies, Chemical Industries, Testing Laboratories and Instrumentation Companies. 03 They can also work as independent Forensic Psychologist Cyber Expert, Questioned Document and Fingerprint Experts and give their valuable opinion to the Court of Law. 04 They would also become eligible to work in Private Forensic Labs as Crime scene investigators, Questioned Document and Fingerprint examiners and Toxicological, anthropological and Serological analysts. 05 They would be equipped with thorough knowledge to clear UGC-NET Examination so that they can pursue a successful career as a researcher. 06 Enhance knowledge, in depth understanding and application of forensic science, policing and criminal investigation by teaching and research. 07 Students will be able to select, interpret and critically evaluate information from a range of sources that include books, scientific reports, journals, case studies and the internet. 08 Develop critical and analytical subject specific skills involving the principles, practices and techniques of specific field of forensic science. 09 They can also analyze the legal, ethical, and constitutional tensions between the interests of society, and the rights of individuals in connection with various criminal procedures and contexts. 10 Students will be able to recognize the need to engage in lifelong learning through continuing education and research. 01 To develop the undergraduate level students with the specific knowledge of handling different types of evidences and their examinations. 02 To develop the laboratory skills in examining different types of digital, biological, physical and chemical evidences found at the crime scene. 03 To prepare the students to compete for employment in the private and government sector (State and Central Forensic Science Labs). 01 Comprehend the need, significance and methodologies of Forensic science investigation alignment with nature and conducive in cultivating skills for successful carrier in research, industry and as an entrepreneurship. 02 Explore scientific skills with a sustainable approach to develop a new innovative solution for merging problems by providing new knowledge in the biological, chemical, physical science field of forensic science.
